TextENGNew: ``Keiichi!@k@vS06/00/152600002. I've been calling you!@k@vS06/00/152600003. You have a phone call.@k@vS06/00/152600004. ...From Sonozaki-san.``
``S-Sonozaki-san... which one?@k@vS06/01/150100181. The older sister?@k@vS06/01/150100182. Or the younger one...?``
``...How should I know.@k@vS06/00/152600006. Go answer it yourself.``
``Shion!@k@vS06/01/150100187. ...Um... about last night... I'm sorry.@k@vS06/01/150100188. ...I lost my temper...``
Cutting the phone off in the middle of Keiichi's rant yesterday seemed to have worked in my favor.
changeReason: Original misrepresents: 'He must have been worried about how I hung up' vs 'Cutting the phone off seemed to have worked in my favor'; Japanese いい影響を及ぼしている means 'having a good/positive effect'

TextENGNew: ``We share the same fate.@k@vS06/06/150602167. Confirming each other's safety is the only guarantee of our own.``
``...Yeah... I agree with that.``
``...That's why we need to share whatever information we have.@k@vS06/06/150602169. ...So we don't end up like Takano-san and the others.``
...Gulp.@k I thought I heard Keiichi swallow on the other end of the receiver.
``Then... let me continue where we left off yesterday.@k@vS06/06/150602171. ...You'll listen without getting angry this time, right?``
Keiichi was quiet and receptive today, listening attentively.
Just as I had the night before, I explained to him how dire his situation was.
That some malicious person or persons in the village might attack us, just as they had attacked Takano-san and the others.
The irony was that the very person issuing these warnings was the one who had painted him as a target for the village's curse system.
changeReason: New translation is more specific: 'painted him as a target for the village's curse system' vs 'made sure the curse would fall upon him'; Japanese 祟りのシステムに彼を目標として認識させた means 'made the system recognize him as a target'

TextENGNew: ``...Let's report to each other if anything seems off.@k@vS06/06/150602173. ...If we piece together the small fragments each of us holds... perhaps we'll discover who killed them... no, not just this year's incidents.``
``We might find the key to solving the entire chain of events from years past.``
``...Mm... you're right.@k@vS06/01/150100193. ...That's a good point.``
``...Then I'll go first, since I suggested it.@k@vS06/06/150602176. ...Lately, I've had the feeling someone has been watching me.``
That was a bluff.@k I just wanted to give Keiichi an even greater sense of urgency.
changeReason: Different word: 'making this up' (fabrication) vs 'a bluff' (strategic deception); Japanese はったり means 'bluff'

TextENGNew: ``...Though it might just be my imagination.@k@vS06/06/150602178. ...But I thought I should mention it anyway.``
``...Right now I think it's just nerves, but... if you've also been feeling like someone's watching you, Kei-chan...@k@vS06/06/150602180. ...then maybe it's not just in our heads...``
``W-Well... don't worry about that.@k@vS06/01/150100196. ...At least on my end... things are fine.``
So at least for today, nothing suspicious had happened around Keiichi.
Now that the heads of all Three Families were gone, the curse system might have already collapsed entirely.
...If so, it would make sense that no one was coming after Keiichi.
But if someone was still targeting Keiichi, it would mean part of the system still survived.
That would mean my vengeance was not yet complete.
changeReason: Significantly different: 'my mission' vs 'my vengeance'; Japanese 仇討ち means 'revenge/vengeance', not 'mission'

TextENGNew: Yes--Keiichi was like a litmus test.
Whether or not enemies took the bait that was Keiichi would tell me if I had wiped them all out.
changeReason: New translation explicitly names Keiichi as 'bait' (餌), matching the Japanese 圭一という餌 which was implicit in the original

TextENGNew: ...What a thoroughly useful and convenient man.
changeReason: New translation adds 'convenient' (便利); Japanese 使い勝手のいい便利な男 means 'convenient and easy-to-use man'

TextENGNew: After that, I steered the conversation along with Keiichi's responses.
I stoked his fear of Mion and amplified his dread of the Three Families, including the Sonozaki family.
I explained the possibility that the entire village was complicit in the conspiracy, and outlined some elements of the system known as Oyashiro-sama's Curse.
Then, remembering that Shion supposedly wouldn't know much about Kimiyoshi's disappearance, I brought it up.
changeReason: New translation adds 'supposedly' capturing という設定を思い出し (remembering the 'setting/pretense'), reflecting the theatrical deception

TextENGNew: ``...I just heard about this a moment ago...@k@vS06/06/150602182. Is it true... that Kimiyoshi-ojii-chan has gone missing...?``
changeReason: Name form change: 'Uncle Kimiyoshi' to 'Kimiyoshi-ojii-chan' matching Japanese 公由のお爺ちゃん throughout dialogue

TextENGNew: ``You mean the mayor...?@k@vS06/01/150100198. Wait, Shion... you haven't heard?``
``I don't know anything! ...I just overheard my father talking about it on the phone again.``

``...Yeah.@k@vS06/01/150100200. Actually... last night, after the meeting, he didn't come home, and the whole village was in an uproar.@k@vS06/01/150100201. They searched everywhere, but I haven't heard that he's been found yet...@k@vS06/01/150100202. The police should be looking for him too, but...``
``Wh-Why didn't you tell me something this important sooner?!!``
``S-Sorry... I thought you already knew...@k@vS06/01/150100204. I wasn't trying to hide it...!``
``............Kei-chan... I... what... what should I do...?``
``...What's wrong?@k@vS06/01/150100206. ...Tell me.@k@vS06/01/150100207. We agreed--no secrets between us, right...?``
``...I'm sorry.@k@vS06/06/150602187. ...I, um... I wasn't trying to hide it.@k@vS06/06/150602188. ...It's just that, well, things got a bit mixed up in order...``
``...We're in this together, right?!@k@vS06/01/150100209. I won't get angry, so just be honest...``
changeReason: New translation adds 'be honest' (正直に) from the Japanese, absent in original

TextENGNew: ``I... I confided in Kimiyoshi-ojii-chan... I told him everything.``
``...Old man Kimiyoshi... he's someone you feel safe confiding in, right, Shion...?``
``...Yes.@k@vS06/06/150602191. ...He always... ever since I was little, he was so kind to me...``
``...Even when I was always playing tricks on him... he would just smile...@k@vS06/06/150602193. ...He'd listen to anything I had to say...@k@vS06/06/150602194. ...He was such a kind person...``
``...C-Calm down, Shion.@k@vS06/01/150100212. ...It's not like he's dead, right?@k@vS06/01/150100213. Don't give up hope so easily...``
I could tell Keiichi was trying to cheer me up, hearing the dejection in my voice.
changeReason: New translation adds Shion's self-awareness of her acting: 落ち込んだ風な声 (voice sounding dejected), absent from original

TextENGNew: But his words had no substance behind them.
changeReason: Different criticism: 'he had no idea what was actually going on' vs 'his words had no substance'; Japanese 何の裏付けもない means 'no backing/evidence'

TextENGNew: Knowing the truth, his attempts were almost comically hollow.
changeReason: Different rendering: 'It really made me laugh' vs 'his attempts were almost comically hollow'; Japanese こんなにも滑稽なものなのか means 'is it this comical/absurd?'

TextENGNew: ``...I told the mayor...@k@vS06/06/150602196. ...that night...@k@vS06/06/150602197. I confessed that I snuck into the ritual storehouse.@k@vS06/06/150602198. ...That someone saw us... and that there were people out to get us.``
``...Does the mayor know how Takano-san and the others actually died...?``

``...Yes.@k@vS06/06/150602200. ...He knew.@k@vS06/06/150602201. ...I told him those two died from Oyashiro-sama's curse... and that I might be made a sacrifice to appease the anger.@k@vS06/06/150602202. ...I was completely frank with him.``

``...Kimiyoshi-ojii-chan... didn't get angry.@k@vS06/06/150602204. ...He smiled and said, if Shion-chan truly feels remorse, there's no way she'll be demoned away...@k@vS06/06/150602205. ...He was really... smiling... and told me to leave it to him...... ...ngh...!!``
changeReason: Name form: 'Uncle Kimiyoshi' to 'Kimiyoshi-ojii-chan' matching Japanese; consistent throughout

TextENGNew: As I spoke these words myself, I actually felt a pang of sadness.
Kimiyoshi-ojii-chan, who had seemed so dependable.
...When he said he would protect Shion, I had been happy from the bottom of my heart.@k ...So happy...
changeReason: New translation adds 心の底から (from the bottom of my heart), intensifying the emotion as in the Japanese

TextENGNew: ...If only he hadn't gone on to say those cold things about Satoshi-kun...!!
changeReason: Different characterization: 'insult Satoshi-kun' vs 'say those cold things about Satoshi-kun'; Japanese 冷たく扱う means 'treat coldly', not 'insult'

TextENGNew: ``.........It's my fault.@k@vS06/06/150602207. ...Because I... confessed everything to him...``
``...Stop it, Shion...@k@vS06/01/150100218. ...It's not your fault.``

``No!!@k@vS06/06/150602209. It IS my fault!!@k@vS06/06/150602210. Because I... told him everything...!@k@vS06/06/150602211. Because Kimiyoshi-ojii-chan... found out...!@k@vS06/06/150602212. That's why... he must have been killed!!``
``I mean, it was right after I confessed to him!@k@vS06/06/150602214. He disappeared that very night, right after telling me everything would be all right!!!``
It was a strange sensation.
I wasn't actually confused at all.
But as I pretended to be distraught and murmured prayers for Kimiyoshi's safety, those very emotions began sprouting within me simultaneously.
I was only acting out these intense feelings to deceive Keiichi,@k yet the emotions I was performing began overflowing in reverse... until I felt myself being swallowed by them.
So the words spilling from my mouth... weren't words I was saying deliberately.
...They were words being spoken by the emotion that had consumed me.
I wasn't saying anything, yet someone other than me was speaking freely.
changeReason: Different description: 'someone else inside of me was doing the talking' vs 'I wasn't saying anything, yet someone other than me was speaking'; Japanese 私は何も口にしていないのに clarifies 'I wasn't saying anything'

TextENGNew: ...I was merely watching from the darkness a short distance away...

``I should never have told him!!!@k@vS06/06/150602216. This was supposed to be our burden alone! Just the four of us!!@k@vS06/06/150602217. It wasn't something to share with anyone else!!@k@vS06/06/150602218. He was killed because I told him!@k@vS06/06/150602219. He was killed because he knew!``
changeReason: Different word: 'our fault' vs 'our burden'; Japanese 咎 means 'sin/blame/burden'

TextENGNew: ``He was killed... because I confessed to him!!!!``

``Wait a minute!!@k@vS06/01/150100220. If someone was doing that... obviously they'd kill you before killing the mayor!@k@vS06/01/150100221. The order doesn't make sense!@k@vS06/01/150100222. If anyone should be killed first, it's you or me!!@k@vS06/01/150100223. Why would other people die before us?!!``
``There IS an order!!@k@vS06/06/150602222. They must be planning to kill us last of all!!``
changeReason: Different meaning: 'It does make sense!' vs 'There IS an order!'; Japanese 順番はあります literally means 'there is an order/sequence'

TextENGNew: ``...Instead of killing us outright... they kill the people close to us one by one... and only after we've suffered as much grief as possible do they finally kill us... that must be their plan!!``
changeReason: New translation adds 'Instead of killing us outright' (ひと思いに殺さないで) and 'one by one' (順々に), details from the Japanese absent in original

TextENGNew: ...What I had just said startled even me.
Kill our loved ones first, make us suffer, and only then...
...Whoaaa... that's amazing, that's incredible, that's terrifying...
changeReason: Very different: 'I felt something crawling up my spine' (physical sensation) vs 'that's amazing, incredible, terrifying' (excitement); Japanese すごいすごい、すげえよそれ expresses admiration/excitement

TextENGNew: I really am no longer human. I really am a demon.
No human mind could conceive of something so cruel...!!
And the thing is, this plan--
changeReason: Completely different sentence: 'Why was I getting so excited about this?' vs 'And the thing is, this plan--'; Japanese しかもさ、この方法 means 'moreover, this method/plan'

TextENGNew: ...I Could Put It Into Action Immediately!!@k Heh heh heh heh heh, heh heh heh!!!
changeReason: New translation uses capitalization to represent the Japanese katakana スグニ実行可能ナンダヨ which indicates altered mental state

TextENGNew: ``I... I also... actually... I... I told her.@k@vS06/01/150100229. ...I told Rika-chan... today!!``
``Rika-chan... you mean Rika-chama?@k@vS06/06/150602227. The one from Furude Shrine?``
``Yes, Rika-chan!@k@vS06/01/150100231. ...I was so... so anxious and scared... that I told her.@k@vS06/01/150100232. ...I told Rika-chan everything.``
``...And then... what did Rika-chama say...?``
``...The kitty cat is worrying too much.@k@vS06/01/150100234. I'll definitely do something about it for you.``
changeReason: Different Rika dialogue: 'The cat' vs 'The kitty cat' (猫さん); also 'for sure' vs 'definitely for you' -- adding あげます (for you)

TextENGNew: ``That's what Rika-chan said...!!@k@vS06/01/150100236. ...I-I'm sorry, Shion!@k@vS06/01/150100237. I... I'm... getting really worried about Rika-chan...!!``
``Oh, yes! ...If you're that worried, please go check on her right away.@k@vS06/06/150602230. Please confirm that she's safe.``
``I'll call you again tomorrow at around this same time.@k@vS06/06/150602232. If I do, you'll know I'm still safe.``
Keiichi slammed the receiver down.

changeReason: New translation adds 'Pfft' (ぷ), the sound of suppressed laughter present in Japanese but missing from original

TextENGNew: Phone calls with Keiichi were endlessly entertaining.
Everything about it was hilarious. Absolutely delightful.
Keiichi Maebara.@k Go ahead and struggle to your heart's content.
...Run around the village as you please and test whether the curse still lives.
Check for me whether any of my enemies remain!
After cackling for a good while, I cooled my thoughts.
I had already disposed of the bicycles and footwear Rika and Satoko had come on.
The house was taken care of, for now.
...The likely developments going forward: Keiichi would raise a fuss, consult someone... and at some point, the matter would come to Mion.
changeReason: Different subject: 'come to Mion' (Keiichi goes to Mion) vs 'the matter would reach Mion' (information reaches Mion); Japanese 魅音に相談が来る means 'consultation comes to Mion'

TextENGNew: Then, just like last night, they'd probably organize the youth group to search the village.
...Another late night ahead. There's nothing more exhausting than searching for something you know will never be found.
Well then, the call shouldn't be long in coming.
changeReason: Different statement: 'I decided to wait' vs 'the call shouldn't be long in coming'; Japanese そう長い時間も経たない内に連絡が来よう means 'a call should come before long'

TextENGNew: I killed time watching TV, waiting leisurely for the phone to ring.
``...Kei-chan, are you serious?@k@vS06/06/150602236. If this is a joke, I'm going to be really angry.``
When Keiichi voiced his suspicion that Rika had disappeared, I scolded him for saying something so imprudent.
``Mii-chan.@k@vS06/02/150200196. If it really were a joke, you shouldn't get mad--you should laugh.@k@vS06/02/150200197. Because it would be a joke, after all.``
``...We need to go verify whether it's a joke or not.``
``...You're right.@k@vS06/06/150602238. ...Sorry for being so irritable.``
Rena had corrected me.
...This girl had an outwardly gentle face, but beneath that thin skin lurked something tremendously frightening.@k ...I had no basis for this feeling, but I felt it all the same.
I'd had this uneasy sense about her ever since that rainy day at the bus stop.
It wasn't that I disliked her or that we didn't get along. Nothing like that.
...How to put it... I couldn't find the right words.
...Ah, that's it.@k This feeling--I remember now.
When I was little, I had dreamed of playing tennis.
A relative took me to a tennis court, and I imitated what I saw. They praised me, saying I had talent, and I felt wonderful.
In reality, I was just swatting at a ball with a racket--no different from a child playing catch.@k But being told I had talent made me incredibly happy.
So when school started again after the holiday, I proudly boasted that I could play tennis and had been praised for my talent.
...And then.@k A girl who was normally unremarkable--no distinguishing features, someone whose presence or absence made little difference--stepped forward and said, 'I play too.'
And not just 'playing'--she had a proper coach and was training seriously.@k Compared to her, my bragging about landing most of my serves was laughable.
That feeling of having everyone's attention instantly stolen by someone else, of having my authority humiliated--it was exactly like this.
Someone who normally had no presence at all,@k who turned out to be far more experienced and accomplished than me--the discomfort of discovering that.
Yes, Rena at the bus stop gave me that exact feeling.
Right now, I considered myself a demon.
I believed I had awakened the blood of the half-human, half-demon sages who once dwelt in Onigafuchi Village.
In truth, I had stepped into a world no ordinary human could enter.
And I knew I had already crossed beyond the limits of what a human mind could conceive.
I couldn't deny that I regarded myself as a kind of transcendent being, harboring a certain superiority.
And yet... when my eyes met those of this girl, Rena Ryuugu... I tasted that same emotion from the tennis court.
Why...?
Why did this slip of a girl make me feel like I was about to submit...?
...Come to think of it, I'd heard that animal confrontations are often settled through staring contests and displays of strength.
Humans are the only creatures who need to trade blows to determine dominance.@k Most animals, they say, can gauge each other's strength through a mere exchange of stares.
Then... was that what this was...?!
``...Mii-chan, are you all right?``
``Hmm? Oh, yeah, sorry...!@k@vS06/06/150602240. You know, I've been sleep-deprived since yesterday... sorry...``
Keiichi had propped a ladder against the second-floor window of the storage shed where the two girls lived.
My job was to steady the ladder.@k ...Rena pointed out I was spacing out, snapping me back to attention.
``Mii-chan, I'm going to go check the main house just in case!``
``I'll be right back!``
Rena ran off.
Looking up, I could see Keiichi at the top of the ladder, trying every window, only to find them all locked. He looked completely at a loss.
``...What did she mean by 'main house'?``
Keiichi called down from atop the ladder.
``The Furude family's actual residence.@k@vS06/06/150602244. Apparently it's been left empty ever since her parents passed away.``
``...Ah... right.@k@vS06/01/150100247. ...Rika-chan's parents... they're dead, aren't they.``

``Satoko doesn't have parents either.@k@vS06/06/150602246. ...Her parents fell from a cliff, supposedly from Oyashiro-sama's curse.@k@vS06/06/150602247. ...And her older brother, Satoshi-kun... also disappeared...``
``...Satoshi... I've heard that name before.``
'Heard that name before,' he says...?@k Fury welled up inside me.
The desk you use at school however you please--@k scattering lunch crumbs all over it,@k drooling on it during naps--do you even know whose desk that really is...?!
...Why am I getting worked up? I'm Mion.
...Satoshi-kun's very existence has been erased.
...Because of the curse people. It's only natural Keiichi wouldn't know.@k ...It's not Keiichi's fault...
``Satoko has been living here in this shed with Rika-chan.@k@vS06/06/150602249. Two girls with no family, all alone.@k@vS06/06/150602250. ...Helping each other out.``
``...Didn't someone mention a main house?@k@vS06/01/150100250. Wouldn't it be more comfortable to live there...?``
``Apparently she tried that at first.@k@vS06/06/150602252. ...But she said it was too painful because it reminded her of her parents.``
After being left all alone, Satoko had been living with her best friend Rika.
That's right.@k ...Satoko and Rika were best friends.
Houjou family, Furude family--none of that mattered. They had been best friends since before the dam conflict, from when they were very small.
The reason only Satoko had been spared by the curse.
It was because of Rika Furude's protection, wasn't it?
Yes, that was the most natural conclusion.
Satoko alone, among the cursed Houjou family, had been granted a pardon.@k Through Rika Furude's intervention!
Indeed, Satoko had never been a victim on the night of Watanagashi.

According to the curse system I had unraveled,@k Satoko--the last Houjou survivor, living right in the belly of Hinamizawa, territory of the Three Families--should have been the most obvious target for this year's curse.
So the 'curse executors,' those who sought to please the Three Families, must surely have intended to take her life on the night of this year's Watanagashi.
Yet nothing had befallen Satoko.
This could only mean that Satoko alone had been shielded from the curse's reach.
...That was truly infuriating.
Even though she was merely a child--head of the Furude family or not--Rika had possessed the power to protect her best friend.
...Even as a stand-in, Mion--no, Shion... Shion should have had that same power!!
Just as Rika had shielded Satoko, she could have shielded Satoshi!!@k ...Damn, damn, damn...
``...Rika-chan... and Satoko too... they've had it rough.``
``She's cursed.``
``...Mion... what... did you just say?``
``I said she's cursed.``
``...Mi... Mion, you... what's with that look on your face...?``
``Satoko Houjou.@k@vS06/06/150602256. ...She's a cursed child who bears the full weight of Oyashiro-sama's curse upon her.``
She was a member of the Houjou family, doomed to be swallowed by death's fate, yet Satoko alone had been spared... that was unforgivable...
A member of the cursed Houjou family, of all things...!!
Satoshi-kun hadn't been pardoned, yet Satoko alone had been...!!
She bore the same curse!!@k That she escaped while Satoshi-kun didn't... unforgivable, unforgivable, unforgivable...!!
That's right--the one who most deserved to be cursed in this year of Showa 58 was Satoko!!
She was the one who drove Satoshi-kun to ruin, body and soul!
Because she pushed him so far, everything spiraled out of control, and it all went mad.
Yes--trace every cause and effect back to the source, and Satoko was the most unforgivable of all!!
Because she drove Satoshi-kun into a corner, ..........................., and as a result, Satoshi-kun was erased...!!

She was the origin.@k She was the root cause!!@k If only she hadn't existed!!@k Just as Rika Furude and Satoko lived together happily... perhaps a future of happiness that should never have been possible could have been mine.
Perhaps there was a future where Satoshi-kun and I could have quietly started our lives over in Okinomiya!!@k Yes--connect the beginning to the end, and it's blindingly obvious!

Her very existence was the problem.@k Because she existed, Satoshi-kun was driven to kill his aunt, and the Three Families exploited that, forced him to play a role in the curse, and then erased him...!!!
...The police had written it off as a runaway, but Satoshi-kun wasn't the type to run away.
He always gave his all.
He worked diligently, earnestly, by himself, without relying on anyone.
He had sacrificed everything for his only little sister, and yet he was erased.
He had worked so hard for Satoko!
He had been living solely for that girl, and yet he was erased.
Poor Satoshi-kun.@k How cruelly unrewarded.
What an ungrateful girl.@k ...She was a cursed child.
Whoever drew close to her met the same fate.
Cursed to die,@k or cursed to vanish.
Yes, she was the root of all fate.@k Because she wormed her way into Rika Furude's good graces, the curse fell upon Satoshi-kun instead.@k She should have been the one to die first.
Then Satoshi-kun would still be alive.@k If only he were alive, I could have helped him in any way imaginable!!
Unforgivable, unforgivable--Satoko is unforgivable, that girl is unforgivable, there can be no peace for her, no peace for that girl...
She Was The One Most Befitting This Year's Curse, Showa 58.
Oyashiro-sama's Curse.@k ...Rika Furude, the reincarnation of Oyashiro-sama.
Yes--Rika was dead.
The reincarnation of Oyashiro-sama was dead.
There was no longer a god in this Hinamizawa to deliver the curse.
The right to decide now belonged... to me!
I am Mion Sonozaki, head of the Sonozaki family, one of the Three Families of Onigafuchi!!
Now that all the former Three Families' heads had been eliminated, there was not a single more legitimate successor than me.
Yes--the right to deliver Oyashiro-sama's Curse now belonged to no one but me!
I would deliver the curse.@k I would become the curse.
I was now Oyashiro-sama incarnate!!
From the depths of my body, hot bubbles surged and boiled upward.
I could feel the demon inside me straining to howl.
...Before I knew it, Keiichi was looking down at me.
Calm down, Mion.@k ...As if that dull-witted boy could read my thoughts.@k ...Calm down, calm down.
Just then, I heard many footsteps approaching.
Led by Rena, four or five adults came running up with flashlights in hand...
After that, the storage shed was unlocked and everyone went inside.
Of course, there was no trace of anyone there.
Satoko and Rika had lived here together, just the two of them.
So I doubted Satoko would have left a note saying she was going to the Sonozaki house to visit Rika.
But it would be inconvenient if such evidence existed.
I pushed past Rena, who had gone in first, and searched the room for any such traces.
Rumors of Rika and Satoko's disappearance seemed to be spreading through the neighborhood, and villagers were steadily gathering at the shrine.
Acting as the Sonozaki family head's representative, I issued instructions to split up and search for them.
The villagers nodded at my brisk directives and dispersed.
...The sensation of being Mion, reclaimed after so long.
...I savored a tingling, numbing pleasure...

The shrine grounds were now bustling with villagers coming and going hurriedly,@k calling out that they'd searched here or there to no avail, pointing out places not yet checked, urging each other onward in a noisy clamor.
The women's auxiliary was pulling out large pots, portable gas stoves, and gas canisters from the storage behind the assembly hall, preparing to cook for the searchers.
Amid all this, I noticed Keiichi wobbling unsteadily toward the darkness of the pine grove--perhaps dizzy from the gravity of the situation.
How careless, to wander off alone on a night like this.
...If a 'curse executor' targeting you happened to be nearby, you wouldn't get off lightly...
``Mion-chan, we went all around Onigafuchi Pond but found nothing.@k@vS06/00/villA15002. We shone lights into the water, but we'll need to wait until it's light out to see properly.``
``Thank you for your efforts.@k@vS06/06/150602258. The women's group is making miso soup, so please have some and rest.``
``...Mion-chan, do you know where those two usually parked their bicycles?``
``Hmm.@k@vS06/06/150602260. Probably at the bottom of the shrine stairs, I'd imagine.@k@vS06/06/150602261. They wouldn't carry them all the way up those steps.``
``The thing is, we can't find their bikes.@k@vS06/00/villB15020. Did they ride off somewhere, maybe...?``
``If they went out on their bicycles, they could be far away...@k@vS06/00/villC15002. Town, maybe...?``
``If that's the case, they might not be anywhere in Hinamizawa...``
``By the way, who was the first to notice Rika-chama was missing?``
``Hmm?@k@vS06/06/150602263. Oh, it was Kei-chan.@k@vS06/06/150602264. Said he suddenly had a premonition.``
``...The Maebara boy, huh.@k@vS06/00/villD15003. Hmm, that's a bit suspicious.``
``Come to think of it, where's the Maebara family? At a time like this...``
``The Maebaras haven't joined the neighborhood association, so they're not on the youth group's phone tree.``
``Well, ain't nothing to be done about that.``

``Huh?@k@vS06/00/villD15005. They did join, didn't they?@k@vS06/00/villD15006. Oh, I think it's just that their name didn't make it onto the phone tree yet.@k@vS06/00/villD15007. We only made the list at New Year's.@k@vS06/00/villD15008. They moved here after that, so they're not on it.``
``That Maebara kid's 'premonition' is still mighty suspicious.``
``You know what they say--the person who discovers it first is the most suspicious.``
``Where'd the boy go, anyway?``
``I saw him wandering toward the pine grove a moment ago.@k@vS06/00/villA15004. Should someone go fetch him?``
While we were talking, I spotted Keiichi and Rena returning from the direction of the pine grove.
``...Kei-chan, where did you go?@k@vS06/06/150602266. I was worried you'd disappeared on us too!``
So nothing had happened to Keiichi.
If he was safe, then that was fine.
...It was proof that my vengeance had reached every enemy.
``...Oh, my, my, my...!@k@vS06/11/150700359. I thought I smelled something delicious.@k@vS06/11/150700360. I'd be much obliged if you'd let me have some.``
I heard the thick, oily voice of a middle-aged man.
Ooishi.
...That Ooishi must be thoroughly perplexed by this year's events.
After all, the scale was on an entirely different level from the modest curses of years past.
Ooishi's intelligence network was roughly on par with the board-meeting elders.
In other words, he would already have caught wind of whatever rumors were circulating in the village's underside.
If so, he was probably reading the disappearances of Kimiyoshi and Rika as connected to the atonement for the ritual storehouse lock change by now.
...It wouldn't be long before the police traced their way to me.
I was Mion Sonozaki.
By eliminating my enemies, the heads of the Three Families, I had paradoxically made myself the head.
I could not allow the existence of Mion Sonozaki to continue.
The existence called 'Head of the Sonozaki Family' had to be destroyed.
...Only by destroying my own position would my vengeance be truly complete.
Ooishi would likely be entrusted with the solemn duty of witnessing my final moments...
Our eyes met.
...That Ooishi--he'd already pegged me as the suspect.
That's right--correct answer.@k I'm the culprit.@k Mion Sonozaki, head of the Sonozaki family, is the culprit.@k ...Heh heh heh!!
